title: Command Router
description:
Implement a tiny HTTP-style path router.

Given a path string and an ordered list of route definitions, return the first matching route's name and its captured parameters.

Route pattern syntax:
- Segments are separated by '/'.
- A static segment matches exactly (case-sensitive).
- A ':name' segment matches any non-empty path segment and captures it under the given name.
- A '*' segment must be the last segment. It matches zero or more remaining path segments and captures them as a single string under the key '*'.

Rules:
- Routes are checked in the order provided; the first match wins.
- If no route matches, return None.
- Both path and patterns start with '/'. The root path '/' matches the root pattern '/'.
- Parametric placeholders only match non-empty segments.
function_signature: def match_route(path: str, routes: list[tuple[str, str]]) -> tuple[str, dict[str, str]] | None:
